name: feature-launch-coordination
inputs: Approved feature spec and design mockups
outputs: Feature shipped and live in production

steps.name: Feature specification and design handoff
steps.reason: PM translates customer needs and product strategy into a spec that Engineering can build from. Design and PM iterate until Engineering signals readiness to plan.

steps.name: Validate customer need and market fit
steps.reason: Before investing engineering time, confirm this solves a real problem and aligns with roadmap. This prevents wasted engineering cycles on features nobody needs.

steps.name: Create detailed feature spec with success metrics
steps.reason: Specs define what done looks like. Without this, engineering builds to their interpretation and shipping often reveals misalignment.

steps.name: Engineering capacity planning and estimation
steps.reason: Engineering lead estimates effort, identifies dependencies, and commits to timeline. This is the moment the team becomes accountable for shipping.

steps.name: Break feature into tasks and identify tech dependencies
steps.reason: Large features often depend on other in-flight work or infrastructure changes. Missing dependencies late cause delays.

steps.name: Estimate total engineering effort and define milestone dates
steps.reason: Estimation creates the contract between PM and engineering on what ships and when. Vague timelines cause constant scope creep.

steps.name: Security and compliance review
steps.reason: Security team gates features that touch auth, data, or payments. This is non-negotiable but often causes shipping delays if not started early.

steps.name: Development and QA
steps.reason: Engineering executes the spec. QA validates it matches the spec before shipping. This is where most unknowns surface.

steps.name: Launch readiness review and approval decision
steps.reason: PM makes the final call on whether to ship. Shared responsibility with engineering (if something breaks) and product (if it doesn't move the needle).

steps.name: Production deployment and monitoring
steps.reason: Engineering deploys to production and monitors for errors. If something breaks, they roll back or hotfix. PM is on standby to communicate impact.

steps.name: Post-launch customer communication and feedback collection
steps.reason: Customer success lets customers know the feature is live and collects early feedback. This informs whether the feature solved what PM intended.

steps.name: Post-launch metrics and learning
steps.reason: Tribal knowledge gap: most teams never review post-launch metrics or document what went well and what broke. This prevents learning across launches.
